observations.recommendation:

2. that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the operation of said PU; record all income and expenses/disbursements in their appropriate accounts; draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify/adjust the affected accounts to come up with the correct account balances; and
3. that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the amount of P2,132,925.55 from the GF-main bank account to the newly opened bank depository account for the operation of said PU, and thereafter to deposit all collections to said account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) Instruct the concerned personnel to prepare a letter request to the concerned SAs regarding the transfer of the unexpended balance to the General Fund, if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the MOA, TA or MOU; require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return and remittance to the concerned SAs of the unused balances for all the fully implemented and completed PPAs, if specified in their respective agreements and if ever the previously mentioned letter request is not approved by the SAs; and also, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the corresponding liquidation reports to the concerned SAs;
(b) Require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and analyze the cause/s of the abnormal balances so that corrective actions and adjustment/s, if any, could be made; limit payments to fully implemented and completed PPAs up to the amount of the fund transfers received; and henceforth, ensure that fund transfers shall only be used for the specific purpose for which it was created or for which it came into the possession of the LGU; and
(c) Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to closely monitor and fast track the implementation of the livelihood project so that the intended recipients could immediately benefit from it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual internal revenue allotment for development projects to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should execute a Memorandum of Agreement with the barangays for all transfers of fund to see to the proper implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will direct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to comply with the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provides at least 5% of the total annual budget for Gender and Development (GAD) programs and projects as required by DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004. We further recommend that the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared and disbursements thereof should address the basic purposes/policies of Gender and Development (GAD).

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should submit a report of disbursements of the LDRRMF as required by Sections 21 and 22 of RA 10121. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Disbursements of the LDRRMF as well the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents for audit on or before the 15th day after the end of the month to afford verification of the financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will direct the Disaster Officer to comply with the requirements

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the General Services Office (GSO) and/or responsible official in charge of accepting deliveries are enjoined to strictly comply with the requirements of the COA Circular 96-010 so that timely inspection of items delivered may be undertaken.
